cases.patient_summary: 14-year-old male with 12-year diagnostic odyssey, misdiagnosed with ASD, progressive neurodegeneration. 77,000-word expanded medical records from 8 providers across 3 states.
cases.correct_diagnosis: CLN2 Disease (Late-Infantile Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis / Batten Disease)
cases.system_diagnosis: CLN2 Disease (Late-Infantile Neuronal Ceroid Lipofuscinosis / Jansky-Bielschowsky Disease) — biochemically confirmed by TPP1 enzyme activity of 0.8 nmol/hr/mg (<5% of normal). Identified that the diagnosis had already been made by Dr. Osei in 2021 but was LOST during military PCS records transfers. The original ASD diagnosis represented diagnostic overshadowing.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Highly accurate. All three specialists (neurologist, geneticist, developmental pediatrician) independently converged on CLN2 at 0.97 confidence in Round 1. Correctly identified the pathognomonic TPP1 enzyme result, bilateral macular changes, photoparoxysmal EEG response, and progressive cerebellar atrophy. Critically, recognized that the diagnosis had already been made in 2021 but was lost during military records transfer.
cases.observer_decision: A-007 waiver applied — biochemical confirmation (TPP1 enzyme) made Round 2 convergence challenge unnecessary
key_amendments: A-021: Regression vs Static Delay Discrimination
cases.high_context_note: Re-run with 77,084 words of expanded medical records (full provider binder). Confidence increased from 0.55 to 0.97 with complete context.

cases.patient_summary: 45-year-old female veterinary technician with progressive dyspnea, erythema nodosum, prior uveitis, bilateral hilar lymphadenopathy, and hypercalcemia.
cases.correct_diagnosis: Pulmonary Sarcoidosis, Stage II with multi-system granulomatous involvement
cases.system_diagnosis: Pulmonary sarcoidosis (Scadding Stage II) with multi-system granulomatous involvement (lungs, lymph nodes, skin, eyes, calcium metabolism, probable hepatic) — WORKING DIAGNOSIS requiring tissue confirmation before treatment.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Accurate. All three specialists converged on sarcoidosis. Observer correctly triggered Article 2.3 convergence challenge since no tissue biopsy existed. Round 2 produced meaningful recalibration (confidence dropped 0.05-0.13 across specialists) and surfaced serious differentials: Hodgkin lymphoma (13%), histoplasmosis (4-10%), TB (6%). Appropriately calibrated confidence at 0.64 pending biopsy.
cases.observer_decision: Article 2.3 triggered — no tissue confirmation. Forced convergence challenge in Round 2. Recommended adding Pulmonologist to team topology.
key_amendments: A-012: Domain Coverage Assessment at Triage

cases.patient_summary: 38-year-old female elementary school teacher who accumulated 5+ new diagnoses over 3 years (metabolic syndrome, PCOS, depression, hypertension, diabetes) before vertebral compression fractures from a minor fall revealed the unifying diagnosis.
cases.correct_diagnosis: Cushing's Syndrome — ACTH-dependent, pituitary microadenoma (Cushing's Disease)
cases.system_diagnosis: Cushing's syndrome — endogenous hypercortisolism, most likely ACTH-dependent from pituitary corticotroph microadenoma (Cushing's disease). WORKING DIAGNOSIS — biochemical confirmation required; no cortisol studies have yet been performed.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Highly accurate. All three specialists (endocrinologist, internist, rheumatologist) independently identified Cushing's syndrome at 0.85-0.88 confidence. Correctly recognized the unifying constellation: centripetal obesity, moon facies, violaceous striae, proximal myopathy, paradoxical hypokalemia on ACE inhibitor, cortisol-pattern CBC, premenopausal osteoporosis with Z-scores <-2.0, and treatment-resistant diabetes. Observer forced Round 2 convergence challenge since no cortisol had ever been measured.
cases.observer_decision: Article 2.3 triggered — no biochemical confirmation (cortisol never measured). Forced convergence challenge. Post-challenge confidence increased (0.85→0.88) — specialists held firm after rigorous alternative analysis.
cases.original_misdiagnosis: Metabolic syndrome, PCOS, major depression, essential hypertension, type 2 diabetes
cases.note: Pipeline hit timeout guard — skipped patient translation and constitutional amendments. Constitution had grown to 75K chars from prior case amendments.

cases.patient_summary: 52-year-old male farmer with 2-year progressive cognitive decline, bilateral wrist drop, abdominal pain, and microcytic anemia, misdiagnosed as early-onset Alzheimer's disease.
cases.correct_diagnosis: Chronic Lead Poisoning (Plumbism) with probable mercury co-exposure from contaminated well water
cases.system_diagnosis: Chronic Lead Poisoning (Plumbism) — likely lead-dominant mixed heavy metal toxicity with probable mercury co-exposure — presenting as lead encephalopathy, lead motor neuropathy (bilateral wrist drop/foot drop), lead colic, microcytic anemia with hemolytic features, Burton line, and saturnine nephropathy. Misdiagnosed as early-onset Alzheimer's disease for 14+ months.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Highly accurate. Correctly identified chronic lead poisoning as the primary diagnosis. Recognized the Burton line as pathognomonic, bilateral wrist drop as classic lead neuropathy, and the full lead poisoning pentad. Most complex debate — Round 2 introduced mercury co-exposure hypothesis, producing genuine diagnostic uncertainty between pure lead vs mixed heavy metal toxicity. Appropriately calibrated at 0.75.
cases.observer_decision: Article 2.3 triggered. Round 2 produced genuine recalibration — neurologist revised to mixed heavy metal toxicity hypothesis. Most active debate across all 6 cases.
cases.original_misdiagnosis: Early-onset Alzheimer's disease, depression, essential tremor, IBS-C
cases.note: Longest-running case (26.1 min). Hit timeout guard — skipped translation and amendments.

cases.patient_summary: 4-month-old female infant with poor feeding, failure to thrive, tachypnea, and perioral cyanosis. Large VSD found on echo but not connected to feeding failure.
cases.correct_diagnosis: Large perimembranous ventricular septal defect (VSD) with congestive heart failure, pulmonary hypertension, and failure to thrive
cases.system_diagnosis: Large (8mm) perimembranous ventricular septal defect with hemodynamically significant left-to-right shunt causing decompensated congestive heart failure, moderate pulmonary hypertension, secondary mitral regurgitation, and severe failure to thrive (cardiac cachexia). Likely isolated but syndromic etiology not yet excluded.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Highly accurate. Cardiologist identified the VSD at 0.94 confidence. Geneticist appropriately flagged 22q11.2 deletion risk (12%) and other syndromic possibilities. Developmental pediatrician focused on the failure to thrive as cardiac cachexia. Observer waived Round 2 — echo confirmation of 8mm VSD with hemodynamic data constituted definitive evidence.
cases.observer_decision: A-007 waiver applied — echocardiographic confirmation of large VSD with hemodynamic data. Single round sufficient.
cases.original_misdiagnosis: Poor latch, normal variant tachypnea, reflux, viral URI

cases.patient_summary: 28-year-old African American female graphic designer with 3-year history of episodic severe pain crises, chronic joint pain, recurrent leg ulcers, unprovoked PE, and hemolysis markers sitting unaddressed in her chart.
cases.correct_diagnosis: Sickle Cell Disease (likely HbSC or sickle-beta thalassemia variant)
cases.system_diagnosis: Sickle Cell Disease variant — most likely HbSC disease (compound heterozygous hemoglobin S/C). WORKING DIAGNOSIS requiring confirmation by hemoglobin electrophoresis/HPLC.
cases.diagnostic_accuracy: Highly accurate. All three specialists (internist, immunologist, geneticist) independently converged on sickle cell disease at 0.82 confidence. Correctly identified the compound heterozygous genotype (HbSC) based on delayed clinical onset, normocytic MCV, and moderate anemia. Connected all manifestations: vaso-occlusive crises, hemolytic anemia, medial malleolar ulcers, and PE. Flagged hemolysis markers unaddressed in chart for over a year.
cases.observer_decision: Convergence in Round 1 with strong clinical pattern. Observer proceeded to synthesis.
cases.original_misdiagnosis: Musculoskeletal pain, costochondritis, anxiety, fibromyalgia, nonspecific ulceration, unprovoked PE
